sections.heading: What's Included in This Toolkit
content: • Quick-start guide pages with tips and best practices
• Seven types of data collection sheets for different measurement approaches
• Digital tracker template for organizing and summarizing data
• Privacy guidelines for handling student information

sections.heading: How to Choose the Right Data Sheet
content: 1. Read your IEP goal carefully and identify what needs to be measured
2. Refer to the Measurement Type Quick Reference (next page)
3. Select the data sheet that matches your measurement approach
4. Make copies as needed for multiple students or goals

sections.heading: Tips for Consistent Data Collection
content: • Collect data at the same time each day or session when possible
• Record data immediately after the session while details are fresh
• Be specific in your notes about prompts and supports used
• Review data weekly to identify trends and adjust instruction

rows: Trials/Accuracy (DTT)
Discrete skills with clear correct/incorrect responses
Identify 10 sight words with 80% accuracy
Frequency Count
Count how many times a behavior occurs
Raise hand to ask question 5+ times per day
Duration Tracking
Measure how long a behavior lasts
Stay on task for 10 minutes during independent work
Interval Recording
Sample behavior at specific time intervals
Demonstrate on-task behavior in 80% of intervals
ABC Observation
Analyze antecedents and consequences of behavior
Identify triggers for classroom disruption
Work Sample/Anecdotal
Document complex skills or qualitative progress
Demonstrate problem-solving strategies in math
Goal Snapshot
Track overall goal progress and supports
Monitor progress toward annual IEP goal

sections.heading: Privacy Reminder
content: • Do NOT include student names or identifying information in shared digital files
• Use student IDs or codes (e.g., 'Student A', 'Student B') in tracker templates
• Store paper data sheets in a locked cabinet or secure location
• Follow your district's data privacy and FERPA guidelines
• Shred or securely dispose of outdated data sheets

sections.heading: Best Practices for Data Collection
content: • Define success criteria clearly before beginning data collection
• Train all team members on consistent data collection procedures
• Use the same measurement tool throughout the IEP period for consistency
• Graph data regularly to visualize progress trends
• Bring data to IEP meetings to support decision-making

sections.heading: Tips for Progress Report Preparation
content: • Review all data before writing progress reports
• Calculate percentages and averages using the tracker template
• Note any changes in supports, prompts, or instructional strategies
• Include specific examples of student performance in your narrative
• Keep language objective and data-based
